commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is needed; maintainers need normal merge review plus a decision on whether to land this scoped parser separately from the open file-share rich-object parser.; Cleared: The diff adds signed-webhook payload parsing and tests only; it introduces no new dependency, network call, credential handling, or broader command execution surface. (sha=8106ac957ddf0deb9dc07d4153cb9869a5e2268a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Nextcloud Talk structured webhook body parsing, explicit structured mention matching, effective-body dispatch for command/mention gating, and focused parser/delivery-policy tests.

PR surface: Source +135, Tests +358. Total +493 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main passes raw structured JSON text into mention regex matching and command/envelope fields, while the linked issue and PR proof show Talk sends mention placeholders in structured payloads.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; maintainers need normal merge review plus a decision on whether to land this scoped parser separately from the open file-share rich-object parser.

target.title: fix(nextcloud-talk): parse structured mention payloads
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem 1 — mention parsing:** Nextcloud Talk webhooks deliver messages as structured JSON (`{"message":"hi {mention-user1} !","parameters":{"mention-user1":{"type":"user","id":"agent",...}}}`) per the documented bot API spec — not plain text. The inbound handler was passing `object.content` raw to mention regex matching and command gating, so patterns never fired against the JSON envelope.
- **Problem 2 — group reply delivery:** Group chats default to `message_tool_only` delivery mode, which suppresses the LLM's text reply and expects the agent to call the `message` tool instead. The nextcloud-talk channel does not define applicable tool actions for the group reply context, so the tool list was empty and every reply was silently dropped — the response appeared only in the web UI, never reaching Nextcloud Talk.
- **Why it matters:** Agents in group rooms with `requireMention: true` silently drop every message, even direct @-mentions. When they do process a message, the reply never reaches the Nextcloud Talk room.
- **What changed:**
  - Added `parseStructuredNextcloudTalkBody` to extract the human-readable `message` field and decode `parameters` into typed mention entries.
  - Added `resolveExplicitNextcloudTalkMention` to check whether any `type: "user"` entry matches the configured account id, API user, or display name.
  - Structured bodies with only mention placeholders and no actionable text are dropped silently.
  - The effective plain-text body is used for command gating and agent dispatch (fixing `body: rawBody` → `body: effectiveBody` in the agent envelope).
  - Added `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "automatic"` to the inbound `replyOptions` so group replies are delivered directly to the room instead of waiting for a message tool call that never comes.
- **What did NOT change:** Auth flow, allowlists, group/DM policy, pairing, session routing. Plain-text bodies continue to work unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression
	- https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/66700

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: agents in Nextcloud Talk group rooms never responded to @-mentions, and when they did process a message the reply was never delivered back to the group room
- Real environment tested: Nextcloud 32 / Talk 21, Linux, Node 24, OpenClaw v2026.5.6
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: configured group room with `requireMention: true`, sent a message @-mentioning the bot, observed reply in Nextcloud Talk
- Evidence after fix: gateway runtime logs confirm inbound structured body parsed and reply delivered to group room (room token `iodwj5dz`):
  ```
  May 08 16:29:46 aeris node[1239189]: rawBody="{\"message\":\"{mention-user1} test message\",...}" effectiveBody="test message" structured=true
  ```
  Bot reply appeared in the Nextcloud Talk group room within seconds of the message being sent. Before the fix, the reply appeared only in the OpenClaw web UI and never reached Nextcloud Talk.
- Observed result after fix: agent correctly detects @-mentions in structured JSON payloads and delivers the reply directly to the Nextcloud Talk group room
- What was not tested: Talk versions prior to 21; alternative structured payload shapes if any exist

## Root Cause

- **Root cause 1:** The Nextcloud Talk bot webhook spec documents `object.content` as a JSON-encoded string with `message` and `parameters` keys. The handler was written assuming plain text and matched mention regexes directly against the raw JSON string.
- **Root cause 2:** Group chats use `message_tool_only` delivery mode by default (see `source-reply-delivery-mode.ts`). The mode suppresses the LLM's final text reply and expects the agent to invoke the `message` tool. Because nextcloud-talk does not register applicable tool actions for the group-reply context, the tool list sent to the model was empty, the model returned plain text, and that text was suppressed. DMs were unaffected because they default to `automatic` mode.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No test covered the structured-body path or group reply delivery path.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `extensions/nextcloud-talk/src/inbound.mentions.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: structured JSON body with `type: "user"` mention entries triggers `resolveExplicitNextcloudTalkMention` and routes correctly; plain-text bodies pass through unchanged; malformed JSON falls back gracefully; mention-only bodies (empty after stripping) are dropped.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the parsing and mention resolution logic is pure and fully exercisable without a live Nextcloud instance.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Agents in Nextcloud Talk group rooms now correctly detect explicit @-mentions delivered via the structured JSON format and deliver replies back to the Nextcloud Talk room. Previously every group message was silently dropped (mention parsing), and even when processed, the reply never reached Nextcloud Talk (delivery suppression).

## Diagram

```text
Before:
object.content (JSON string) -> rawBody -> matchesMentionPatterns(rawBody) -> no match -> drop

After:
object.content (JSON string) -> parseStructuredNextcloudTalkBody -> effectiveBody (plain text) + mentionEntries
  -> resolveExplicitNextcloudTalkMention(mentionEntries) OR matchesMentionPatterns(effectiveBody)
  -> match -> dispatch -> sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "automatic" -> deliverNextcloudTalkReply -> Nextcloud Talk API
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (Debian)
- Runtime/container: Node 24
- Integration/channel: Nextcloud Talk (Nextcloud 32, Talk 21)

### Steps

1. Configure a Nextcloud Talk group room with `requireMention: true`
2. Send a message @-mentioning the agent
3. Before fix: message silently dropped, no reply anywhere. With partial fix (mention parsing only): message processed, reply visible in OpenClaw web UI only. After full fix: reply delivered to Nextcloud Talk group room.

### Expected

Agent receives and processes the @-mentioned message and replies in the Nextcloud Talk room.

### Actual (before fix)

Message dropped — mention regex matched against raw JSON envelope, never found a match. Group reply delivery silently suppressed by `message_tool_only` mode.

## Evidence

- [x] 16 unit tests added in `inbound.mentions.test.ts`, all passing (`pnpm test extensions/nextcloud-talk`: 70/70 after both fixes)
- [x] Live verification on Nextcloud 32 / Talk 21 — bot replied in group room after patch

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: @-mention in a live Nextcloud Talk group room correctly triggers agent response and reply is delivered to the room.
- Edge cases checked: plain-text body pass-through, malformed JSON fallback, `type != user` guard, case-insensitive id matching, email local-part matching, mention-only body drop, DM delivery unaffected.
- What you did **not** verify: Talk versions prior to 21.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes — plain-text bodies continue to work unchanged; JSON bodies are parsed transparently; DM delivery unchanged.
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Nextcloud Talk changes the structured payload format in a future release.
  - Mitigation: `parseStructuredNextcloudTalkBody` falls back to the raw body string on any parse failure, preserving pre-fix behavior.
- Risk: `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "automatic"` bypasses message-tool delivery for group rooms.
  - Mitigation: nextcloud-talk bots always reply to the triggering room — there is no cross-channel or proactive-delivery use case here. The message tool path adds no value and actively breaks delivery.
target.author: iclem

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
